name: Benasquese (patués, Benasque valley)

ancestors.notes: Aragonese (Ribagorzano High-Aragonese) stock: benasqués (patués, the speech of the Benasque valley / Vall de Benás, Ribagorza, Huesca) inherits the core Aragonese grapheme→IPA system — five-vowel system with NO Catalan-style vowel reduction, unstressed final vowels PRESERVED (-o, -a, -e not apocopated as in Catalan), Latin F- kept [f], ⟨ch⟩ = [tʃ], palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ = [ʎ] (no yeísmo), and the western-Romance spirantisation of voiced stops.

North-Western (Ribagorçan) Catalan adstrate — benasqués sits on the Aragonese↔Catalan isogloss bundle and is the classic Aragonese-Catalan transitional variety (Saura Rami 2003). Catalan-side traits: a four-degree-of-aperture tonic vowel system parallel to Catalan (closed é/ó vs open è/ò, low functional load); word-final -a → -e before s/n/t in the innermost Zone A (vaca→vaques); and the strongest Catalan alignment in the frontier Zone C (San Feliu de Veri), where diphthongisation recedes. The valley's morphology (article lo/el, els; feminine plural -es in Zone A vs -as elsewhere) is Catalan-leaning but out of grapheme→IPA scope.
Gascon/Occitan Pyrenean adstrate across the Benasque-Luchon pass, inherited via Aragonese.

notes: Benasquese (benasqués, patués, ribagorzano de Benasque) — the Aragonese-Catalan TRANSITIONAL variety of the upper Ésera valley (Vall de Benás/Benasque, Alta Ribagorza, Huesca). Classified by Saura Rami (2003) and most Aragonese/Catalan dialectology as a bridge speech carrying Aragonese structure with Catalan overlay, sitting exactly on the isogloss bundle separating the two languages — the reason the o2i graph needs it as an explicit node rather than folding it into an-x-oriental (Ribagorçan). MODELLED DELTAS over the Aragonese (an) base: (1) SESEO / NO INTERDENTAL: benasqués has NO /θ/ — ⟨z⟩ and ⟨c⟩ before e/i = [s], collapsing the Aragonese/Castilian distinción into a single sibilant. (2) NO VELAR FRICATIVE: benasqués has no /x/; word-initial Latin J-/Ge,i- palatalise to [tʃ] (⟨j⟩ and ⟨g⟩ before e/i = [tʃ]: chemecà, chirà). (3) ⟨x⟩/⟨ix⟩ = [ʃ] (the only prepalatal fricative; system has just /s/ and /ʃ/, NO voiced sibilants /z ʒ dʒ/). (4) ⟨ll⟩ = [ʎ] (also from initial L-: llagó), ⟨ch⟩ = [tʃ]. SEVEN-VOWEL, FOUR-APERTURE tonic system parallel to Catalan (closed é/ó vs open è/ò) — inherited from the an base's e/ɛ, o/ɔ. AGREEMENT WITH THE ARAGONESE KERNEL (inherited, not re-declared): diphthongisation of Latin short Ĕ → ie, Ŏ → ue/ua (the majority living-speech outcome, receding only in frontier Zone C); F- retained [f]; Latin -CT- → -it- (feito, güeit); unstressed final-vowel apocope of -e/-o (Aragonese, contra full Catalan vowel reduction). DOCUMENTED-NOT-MODELLED (out of grapheme→IPA scope or too lexically/positionally conditioned to map cleanly): Zone-A final -a → -e before s/n/t (vaca→vaques); vocalisation of final -B/-C/-D/-V → glide (nèu, perdiu); -RN → [rt] (fort); PL-/CL-/BL-/FL- palatalising clusters (pllanre, cllau); the article paradigm and Zone A/B/C micro-geography. Provenance honesty: base inherited from the Aragonese (an) research-tier spec; benasqués deltas follow Saura Rami (2003) as reported page-by-page in Giralt Latorre's AFA review, cross-checked against the OA Aragonese diachronic phonetics treatise.

sources.author: Saura Rami, J.A.
sources.title: Elementos de fonética y morfosintaxis benasquesas
sources.publisher: Institución Fernando el Católico / Gara d'Edizions, Zaragoza
sources.notes: The reference monograph on Benasquese. Book itself not open; its phonology is reported page-by-page in Giralt Latorre's review (AFA LXI-LXII, pp. 419-424; papers/iberian/afa_LXI-LXII_ebook.pdf), the source used here: 7 vowels / 4 apertures (Saura pp.43-71); Ĕ→ie, Ŏ→ue/ua diphthongisation as the Aragonese kernel (pp.43-59); consonant system keeps /ʎ/, has /ʃ/, has NO /x/, NO /θ/ and NO voiced sibilants /z ʒ dʒ/ (pp.71-106); initial J-/Ge,i- → [tʃ], L- → [ʎ], F- retained; -CT- → [it].

sources.author: Giralt Latorre, J.
sources.title: Reseña de J.A. Saura Rami, Elementos de fonética y morfosintaxis benasquesas
sources.publisher: Archivo de Filología Aragonesa LXI-LXII, pp. 419-424
sources.notes: Read directly (papers/iberian/afa_LXI-LXII_ebook.pdf). Page-by-page summary of Saura's benasqués phonology, used as the OA proxy for the paywalled monograph.

sources.author: Academia Aragonesa de la Lengua (eds.)
sources.title: Para una fonética diacrónica del aragonés
sources.publisher: Revista de la Academia Aragonesa de la Lengua
sources.notes: Read (papers/iberian/aragones_fonetica_diacronica.pdf). Cross-check for the pan-Aragonese -CT- > -it-, F- retention, and initial L-/N- and G/J palatalisation shared by benasqués.

sources.author: Nagore Laín, F.
sources.title: Gramática de la Lengua Aragonesa
sources.notes: Aragonese reference grammar; benasqués placed among the eastern (Ribagorzan) High-Aragonese valley speeches.

location.notes: Benasque (Benás), upper Ésera valley, Alta Ribagorza, Huesca, Aragón.
